title: Sign and Verify an Artifact
description:
## Prove Integrity With a Signature

Artifact signing is the foundation of supply-chain trust: you sign with a private key, and anyone can verify with the public key.

A file to sign is at **`/root/message.txt`**.

**Your task:**
1. generate an RSA private key at **`/root/priv.pem`**
2. extract its public key to **`/root/pub.pem`**
3. create a SHA-256 signature of `message.txt` at **`/root/sig.bin`**

The check verifies the signature with the public key — it must report **`Verified OK`**.

> 💡 `openssl genrsa`, then `openssl rsa -pubout`, then `openssl dgst -sha256 -sign ...`.

hints.title: Key, public key, signature
hints.body: Generate the private key, derive the public key, then sign the file's SHA-256 digest with the private key.
hints.command: openssl genrsa -out /root/priv.pem 2048 && openssl rsa -in /root/priv.pem -pubout -out /root/pub.pem && openssl dgst -sha256 -sign /root/priv.pem -out /root/sig.bin /root/message.txt

hints.title: Verify it yourself
hints.body: Verification uses the public key and the signature against the original file.

validation.description: Checks that the signature verifies against the public key for message.txt.
